earlyVoting.notes: Early voting is at least 8 days and up to 14 days before the election. Exact dates and hours set by each county supervisor of elections. TODO verify for 2026 cycle.
Early voting is at least 8 days before the election. Exact dates and hours set by each county supervisor of elections. TODO verify for 2026 cycle.
Early voting is at least 8 days before the election. Exact dates and hours set by each county supervisor of elections. TODO verify for 2026 cycle.

acceptedIds: Florida ID card issued by DHSMV
Debit or credit card with photo
Veteran health identification card issued by the U.S. Department of Veterans Affairs
votingRules.expirationRule: Photo ID must not be expired, unless it is a U.S. military ID, in which case it need not contain an expiration date. TODO verify for 2026 cycle.
votingRules.impedimentDeclaration: If a voter does not have an acceptable photo ID, they may cast a provisional ballot and present acceptable ID to the supervisor of elections by 5 p.m. on the second day after the election.
votingRules.phonesAtPollsDetail: Florida does not prohibit phones at polling places, but voters may not use phones in a way that interferes with the election process. Photography that reveals another voter's ballot is prohibited.
additionalRules: Closed primary: only voters registered with a party may vote in that party's primary. No-party-affiliation (NPA) voters cannot vote in partisan primaries unless all candidates in a race are from one party.
No runoffs in Florida general elections — the candidate with the most votes wins.

eligibility: Any registered voter may request a vote-by-mail ballot without providing a reason
voteByMail.applicationDeadline: No later than 7 days before the election (received by the supervisor of elections)
voteByMail.returnDeadline: Must be received by the supervisor of elections no later than 7 p.m. on Election Day
voteByMail.returnDeadlinePlain: Must arrive at the supervisor of elections office by poll closing on Election Day. Postmarks do not count.

primaryParticipation.ruleExplanationEn: Florida has a closed primary (F.S. §101.021). Only voters registered with a political party may vote in that party's primary. Voters registered with no party affiliation (NPA) or a minor party may not vote in the Republican or Democratic primary unless no other candidates qualify for the general election in that race.
primaryParticipation.ruleExplanationEs: Florida tiene una primaria cerrada (F.S. §101.021). Solo los votantes registrados en un partido político pueden votar en la primaria de ese partido. Los votantes registrados sin afiliación de partido (NPA) o con un partido minoritario no pueden votar en la primaria Republicana o Demócrata, a menos que ningún otro candidato califique para las elecciones generales en esa contienda.
